wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the project overview section focused on the Execution Information Query System. Explain its purpose as an automated CLI tool for querying China's Execution Information Public Disclosure Network, its core functionality for multi-subsite data extraction, and relationship to web scraping automation. Document the system architecture, target audience, and use cases. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including zhixing, shixin, and xgl subsites. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like batch queries and daily workflows. Document the overall system design, dependencies, and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the introduction and purpose section focused on the Execution Information Query System. Explain the project's mission to automate querying China's Execution Information Public Disclosure Network (zxgk.court.gov.cn) for three subsites: zhixing (executed persons), shixin (dishonest executed persons), and xgl (restricted consumption personnel). Document the system's role in providing automated, reliable, and efficient access to execution information for businesses, legal professionals, and researchers. Include the ethical and legal considerations surrounding web scraping, the target audience (businesses, law firms, compliance teams), and the value proposition of automating repetitive data collection tasks. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and provide context for why this tool was developed.

wiki_catalogs.name: OCR Service API Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the OCR service endpoints. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication methods for the CAPTCHA solving service. Include detailed specifications for /solve (multipart upload), /solve/text (plain text response), and /solve/base64 (Base64 input) endpoints. Document request parameters including preprocess modes (full, gray, none), response models with success flags, confidence scores, and elapsed timing. Include authentication methods, CORS configuration, and error handling strategies. Provide practical examples of API usage with curl commands and client implementation guidelines. Address rate limiting considerations and service availability checks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Writer Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the writer architecture overview focused on the pluggable writer system design. Explain the factory pattern implementation, unified interface specification, and module organization. Document the write() method contract, batch processing workflow, and command-line execution patterns. Include practical examples of writer registration, initialization, and configuration. Explain the relationship between the main application and individual writers, error propagation mechanisms, and performance considerations. Address extensibility patterns for adding new output formats and integration with the main query engine.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stealth Browser Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the stealth browser configuration system focused on Playwright browser initialization and anti-detection measures. Explain the BrowserManager class implementation, including constructor parameters, stealth settings, and browser launch configurations. Document the BROWSER_ARGS array with detailed explanations of each Chrome flag and its security implications. Cover stealth.js integration through playwright-stealth library, including navigator platform override, WebGL vendor spoofing, and language header manipulation. Include concrete examples of browser context creation, viewport configuration, and locale settings. Address proxy environment variable handling, process cleanup mechanisms, and signal handling for graceful shutdown. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common browser initialization issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Browser Automation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the browser automation system that powers the Execution Information Query System. Detail the BrowserManager class architecture including stealth configuration with playwright-stealth, process cleanup mechanisms, and multi-subsite navigation patterns. Document the stealth browser setup with custom headers, viewport configuration, and automation detection evasion techniques. Explain the WAF detection and bypass mechanisms, including CSS selector-based navigation, DOM element validation, and automatic retry logic. Cover the browser lifecycle management, orphan process cleanup, and signal handling for graceful shutdown. Include practical examples of browser initialization, navigation sequences, and error recovery strategies. Address performance considerations, memory management, and resource cleanup patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: OCR Service API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the OCR service endpoints. Document all HTTP methods and URL patterns including GET / (root), GET /health (health check), POST /solve (multipart/form-data upload), POST /solve/text (plain text response), and POST /solve/base64 (Base64 input). For each endpoint, specify request/response schemas using Pydantic models, parameter descriptions, validation rules, and error handling. Include detailed examples of request formats, response structures, and common use cases. Document pre-processing modes (full, gray, none), file size limits, image dimensions, and CORS configuration. Provide client implementation guidelines, performance considerations, and troubleshooting tips for common issues like invalid file types, size limits, and processing errors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the getting started section focused on installation and initial setup of the Execution Information Query System. Explain the prerequisite requirements including Python 3.10+, memory requirements, and OCR service dependencies. Document the step-by-step installation process using setup.sh, configuration of environment variables, and creation of company lists. Include practical examples of first query execution and environment preparation. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common installation issues. Address both manual setup and automated installation approaches. Make content accessible to beginners while ensuring all technical requirements are covered.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Requirements and Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the system requirements and dependencies section focused on the Execution Information Query System. Document hardware requirements (minimum 4GB RAM), operating system compatibility (Ubuntu, macOS), and software prerequisites (Python 3.10+, npm, Docker). Detail the OCR service requirements and explain the dual approach: built-in PaddleOCR installation via setup.sh or external OCR service deployment. Document all Python dependencies including Playwright, FastAPI, PaddleOCR, OpenCV, and Feishu SDK. Include memory considerations for OCR models and browser automation. Provide installation verification steps and dependency validation procedures. Address optional components and their impact on system performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Solver Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the CAPTCHA solver implementation. Thoroughly explain the CaptchaSolver class architecture, PaddleOCR integration, and text extraction algorithms. Document the solve_captcha_from_bytes function including confidence threshold validation, retry mechanisms, and error handling strategies. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing solver initialization, image processing workflows, and result interpretation. Document configuration options for OCR models, preprocessing parameters, and performance tuning. Explain relationships with preprocessing pipeline and integration with main query system. Address common OCR issues, accuracy optimization, and fallback strategies when recognition fails.

wiki_catalogs.name: SQLite Database Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the SQLite integration writer focused on database schema design and data persistence. Explain the execution record storage format, table creation and migration strategies, and data validation rules. Document the write() method implementation, batch insertion optimization, and error handling during database operations. Include practical examples of database queries, data retrieval patterns, and backup procedures. Explain indexing strategies for performance optimization, concurrent access handling, and data integrity constraints. Address database maintenance tasks, schema evolution, and compatibility considerations across different SQLite versions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Subsite Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the multi-subsite navigation system focused on seamless transition between zhixing, shixin, and xgl subsites. Thoroughly explain the navigate() method implementation, including subsite configuration loading, CSS selector-based link targeting, and navigation state management. Document the _click_subsite() method with CSS selector patterns and DOM interaction strategies. Cover the subsite-specific navigation differences, particularly the shixin special case requiring province selection. Include concrete examples of navigation flow, error handling for failed navigations, and retry mechanisms. Explain the diagnose() method for system health verification and DOM structure validation. Address common navigation issues like selector updates due to website changes and performance considerations for rapid subsite switching.

wiki_catalogs.name: CAPTCHA Solving System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the CAPTCHA solving system architecture and integration patterns. Explain the integration with the standalone OCR service (captcha-solver), including API endpoints, request/response formats, and error handling strategies. Cover the image preprocessing pipeline, base64 encoding/decoding, and canvas-based image extraction from the verification code container. Detail the retry mechanisms, confidence threshold validation, and fallback strategies for failed OCR attempts. Include practical examples of captcha detection, extraction, solving workflows, and integration with the browser automation system. Address performance optimization, timeout handling, and service availability checks.

wiki_overview.content:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project structure and implementation. Let me generate the final analysis.

<blog>

# Execution Information Query System - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The Execution Information Query System is a sophisticated automated web scraping solution designed to query China's Execution Information Public Disclosure Network (zxgk.court.gov.cn). The system automates the complex process of searching for legal execution records, handling CAPTCHA challenges, extracting detailed information, and integrating with enterprise collaboration platforms.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Automated Legal Research**: Streamline the process of querying execution information for companies and individuals
- **Robust Anti-Detection**: Implement advanced techniques to bypass WAF protections and anti-bot measures
- **Multi-Format Output**: Support various output formats including JSON, screenshots, and direct database integration
- **Enterprise Integration**: Provide seamless integration with Feishu (Lark) multi-dimensional tables for business workflows
- **Reliability**: Implement retry mechanisms, progress tracking, and error recovery systems

### Target Audience
- Legal professionals conducting due diligence
- Compliance teams monitoring corporate risks
- Business analysts tracking execution patterns
- Enterprise users requiring automated data collection workflows

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[CLI Interface] --> B[Browser Manager]
    B --> C[Captcha Solver Service]
    C --> D[Query Engine]
    D --> E[Result Processing]
    E --> F[Output Writers]
    
    G[Configuration] --> A
    H[Company Lists] --> A
    I[Feishu Integration] --> F
    
    J[OCR Engine] --> C
    K[OpenCV] --> E
    L[Playwright] --> B
```

### Design Patterns

**Module-Based Architecture**: The system follows a clear modular design with distinct responsibilities:
- **Browser Management**: Handles WebDriver lifecycle and stealth techniques
- **Captcha Processing**: Manages OCR services and image preprocessing
- **Data Extraction**: Implements robust DOM parsing and data validation
- **Output Generation**: Supports multiple output formats and destinations

**State Machine Pattern**: The query process follows a well-defined state progression:
- Initialization → Navigation → Authentication → Query → Result Collection → Output

**Factory Pattern**: Multiple output writers support different target systems (Feishu, Excel, SQLite)

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CLI as CLI Interface
    participant BM as Browser Manager
    participant CS as Captcha Solver
    participant QE as Query Engine
    participant FS as Feishu Writer
    
    CLI->>+BM: Launch Browser
    BM->>+CS: Health Check
    CS-->>-BM: OK
    BM->>+QE: Execute Query
    QE->>+CS: Extract & Solve CAPTCHA
    CS-->>-QE: OCR Result
    QE->>QE: Parse Results
    QE-->>-CLI: Results
    CLI->>+FS: Write to Feishu
    FS-->>-CLI: Success/Failure
    CLI->>-BM: Cleanup
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Primary CLI**: `zxgk_query.py` - Main application entry point with comprehensive command-line interface
- **Diagnostic Tool**: `diagnose_subsites.py` - Automated DOM structure probing for all three subsites
- **Setup Script**: `setup.sh` - Complete installation and dependency management
- **Daily Workflow**: `cron_daily_query.sh` - Scheduled execution wrapper

### Core Modules

#### Browser Management (`BrowserManager`)
Handles Chrome automation with advanced anti-detection techniques:
- Stealth mode configuration with custom user agents
- Process cleanup to prevent orphaned browser instances
- Multi-subsite navigation with CSS selector-based targeting
- WAF detection and automatic retry mechanisms

#### CAPTCHA Processing (`CaptchaSolver`)
Advanced OCR integration with multiple processing modes:
- Base64 image processing for remote OCR services
- Automatic image extraction from DOM elements
- Confidence threshold validation
- Retry logic for failed OCR attempts

#### Query Engine (`QueryEngine`)
Robust search execution with comprehensive error handling:
- Dynamic form field detection and population
- Pagination handling with viewId deduplication
- Modal dialog dismissal for consistent data access
- Cross-site compatibility across zhixing, shixin, and xgl subsites

#### Output Writers (`writers/` package)
Flexible output generation supporting multiple destinations:
- **Feishu Integration**: Direct multi-dimensional table updates
- **Excel Export**: Structured spreadsheet generation
- **SQLite Storage**: Local database persistence
- **JSON Serialization**: Standardized data interchange format

### Configuration Approach
- **YAML Configuration**: Centralized settings management with environment variable expansion
- **Subsite Configuration**: Modular subsite definitions with CSS selectors
- **Feature Flags**: Toggle-based feature control (screenshots, cross-references)
- **Rate Limiting**: Configurable delays and retry limits

### External Dependencies
- **Playwright**: Modern browser automation with stealth capabilities
- **FastAPI**: High-performance OCR service backend
- **PaddleOCR**: Advanced optical character recognition
- **OpenCV**: Computer vision for image processing
- **Feishu SDK**: Enterprise collaboration platform integration

### Integration Points
- **Feishu Multi-dimensional Tables**: Real-time data synchronization
- **OCR Services**: Remote CAPTCHA solving capabilities
- **File Systems**: Local storage for intermediate results
- **Network APIs**: RESTful interfaces for external communication

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class BrowserManager {
        +launch()
        +navigate(subsite)
        +diagnose(subsite)
        -_cleanup_orphans()
    }
    
    class CaptchaSolver {
        +health_check()
        +get_captcha(page)
        +solve(base64)
        +refresh(page)
    }
    
    class QueryEngine {
        +query(company)
        -_collect_all_pages()
        -_dismiss_dialogs()
    }
    
    class BatchRunner {
        +run(companies)
        -save_result()
        -_mark_progress()
    }
    
    class FeishuWriter {
        +write_query_results()
        +upload_screenshots()
    }
    
    BrowserManager --> CaptchaSolver : uses
    QueryEngine --> CaptchaSolver : uses
    BatchRunner --> BrowserManager : manages
    BatchRunner --> QueryEngine : orchestrates
    BatchRunner --> FeishuWriter : writes to
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Mode Operation**:
- **Single Query Mode**: Individual company searches with immediate results
- **Batch Processing**: Automated bulk queries with progress tracking
- **Backfill Mode**: Post-processing for missing screenshots and data gaps
- **Diagnostic Mode**: System health and DOM structure verification

**Advanced Anti-Detection**:
- Custom browser fingerprinting evasion
- Stealth JavaScript injection for bot detection bypass
- Dynamic viewport and user agent configuration
- Process cleanup to prevent detection artifacts

**Intelligent Data Processing**:
- ViewId-based deduplication across pagination
- Chinese date parsing and timestamp conversion
- Robust error recovery with configurable retry limits
- Progress tracking for long-running operations

### Implementation Highlights

**Modular Architecture** (`zxgk_query.py`):
- Clear separation of concerns across 8+ distinct modules
- Comprehensive exception handling with specific error types
- Configurable retry mechanisms with exponential backoff
- Memory-efficient processing for large datasets

**OCR Integration** (`captcha-solver/main.py`):
- FastAPI-based service with CORS support
- Multiple input formats (multipart, base64)
- Performance metrics and logging
- Graceful degradation for partial failures

**Enterprise Integration** (`writers/feishu.py`):
- Direct Feishu API integration via lark-cli
- Cross-reference matching between tables
- Batch processing with rate limiting
- Media upload optimization for large files

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialized
    Initialized --> BrowserLaunched: Setup Complete
    BrowserLaunched --> Navigation: Navigate to Subsite
    Navigation --> CaptchaReady: WAF Check Passed
    CaptchaReady --> QueryExecuted: Search Submitted
    QueryExecuted --> ResultsProcessed: Data Extracted
    ResultsProcessed --> OutputGenerated: Formatted Data
    OutputGenerated --> [*]: Complete
    
    Navigation --> WAFBlocked: Detection Failed
    WAFBlocked --> Navigation: Retry After Delay
    
    QueryExecuted --> CaptchaFailed: OCR Error
    CaptchaFailed --> CaptchaReady: Retry OCR
```

**Progress Tracking** (`BatchRunner`):
- JSONL progress files for reliable resume capability
- Automatic checkpoint creation during execution
- Graceful restart handling for interrupted runs
- Summary statistics and completion reporting

**Quality Assurance**:
- Comprehensive diagnostic tools for system verification
- Automated DOM structure probing across all subsites
- Health checks for external dependencies (OCR, Feishu)
- Performance monitoring and logging throughout execution

---

**Sources:**
- [zxgk_query.py](zxgk_query.py)
- [config/zxgk.example.yaml](config/zxgk.example.yaml)

- [captcha-solver/main.py](captcha-solver/main.py)
- [setup.sh](setup.sh)
- [diagnose_subsites.py](diagnose_subsites.py)

</blog>
